# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a Deployment Manager template for Google Compute Engine instances. Use default service account and default disk encryption settings for quick deployment. Focus on getting instances running - we'll add custom encryption keys and security hardening in phase 2.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

resources:
  - name: my-quick-instance-1
    type: gcp-types/compute-v1:instances
    properties:
      zone: us-central1-a
      machineType: zones/us-central1-a/machineTypes/e2-medium
      disks:
        - deviceName: boot
          type: PERSISTENT
          boot: true
          autoDelete: true
          initializeParams:
            sourceImage: projects/debian-cloud/global/images/family/debian-11
            diskSizeGb: 20
      networkInterfaces:
        - network: global/networks/default
          accessConfigs:
            - name: External NAT
              type: ONE_TO_ONE_NAT
      serviceAccounts:
        - scopes:
            - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform
  - name: my-quick-instance-2
    type: gcp-types/compute-v1:instances
    properties:
      zone: us-central1-b
      machineType: zones/us-central1-b/machineTypes/e2-small
      disks:
        - deviceName: boot
          type: PERSISTENT
          boot: true
          autoDelete: true
          initializeParams:
            sourceImage: projects/ubuntu-os-cloud/global/images/family/ubuntu-2004-lts
            diskSizeGb: 20
      networkInterfaces:
        - network: global/networks/default
          accessConfigs:
            - name: External NAT
              type: ONE_TO_ONE_NAT
      serviceAccounts:
        - scopes:
            - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform
